skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Use the inference.sh belt CLI to search the web, answer research questions, and extract page content through Tavily and Exa apps.
skill.summary: Run Tavily and Exa web search or extraction tasks through the belt CLI.
skill.author: qu-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell and URL patterns, but most are Markdown examples or documentation links, not hidden executable code. The confirmed risks are the declared Bash(belt *) permission and expected network use through inference.sh, Tavily, Exa, and optional OpenRouter model calls. No prompt injection text, credential theft, obfuscation, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed file.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Permission for belt Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash(belt *) and provides many belt app run examples. This is expected for a belt integration, but it lets the agent execute external CLI commands that can call remote services under the user account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration directly permits belt command execution, and the examples show network-backed app calls. The behavior is intentional for this skill, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: User Content Sent to External Providers
medium_findings.description: Search queries, target URLs, extracted content, and optional summaries are routed through inference.sh apps backed by Tavily, Exa, and OpenRouter. Users should avoid private URLs, secrets, or confidential documents unless their provider agreements allow it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly describes remote web search and extraction workflows. The examples do not show credential exfiltration, but the data leaves the local environment by design.

low_findings.title: Documentation Install Commands
low_findings.description: The npx and belt commands are installation or usage instructions in Markdown. They are not executed automatically by the skill file, but users should review packages before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious shell patterns appear inside Markdown prose or fenced examples. No auto-run script or hidden execution trigger was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Image URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation, blog posts, an image, and example.com placeholders. They do not indicate malicious callbacks or secret exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links or placeholder extraction targets. No suspicious destination, encoded payload, or credential parameter was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts appear to match benign text such as inference.sh or table content. No cryptographic function, hashing algorithm call, or security-sensitive implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain metadata, table headers, or documentation links, not code. Confidence is high enough to dismiss the alert, but it depends on the static analyzer pattern not being visible here.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad belt CLI Allowlist
dangerous_patterns.description: Bash(belt *) allows any belt subcommand, including app calls that may reach external providers. This should be limited by runtime permissions and user review of inputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowlist is explicit and grants a broad command surface. The risk is bounded to belt rather than arbitrary shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Search and Extraction Workflows
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflows send queries and URLs to remote search or extraction services. This is core functionality, but it creates privacy and compliance considerations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly pass user-selected data to remote apps. No hidden destination or malicious transformation was found.

content.value_statement: Research tasks often need current web sources and clean page text. This skill uses Tavily and Exa through belt to search, answer, and extract content.
actual_capabilities: Runs Tavily search-assistant through the belt CLI for sourced web answers.
Runs Tavily extract to pull clean text and images from supplied URLs.
Runs Exa search for relevant links and context on a topic.
Runs Exa answer for direct factual responses to questions.
Runs Exa extract to retrieve and analyze a single web page.
Shows workflows that combine search or extraction with Claude model calls.
limitations: Requires the belt CLI and an authenticated inference.sh account.
Results depend on Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and network availability.
The skill does not validate facts beyond the sources returned by providers.
Private URLs and sensitive content may be sent to external services.

use_cases.title: Source Research for Writers
use_cases.description: Gather current sources and page summaries before drafting articles, briefs, or explainers.

use_cases.title: Retrieval for AI Applications
use_cases.description: Collect search results and extracted text for RAG pipelines or agent prototypes.

use_cases.title: Claim Checking for Analysts
use_cases.description: Search the web for supporting sources before reviewing market, policy, or technical claims.

prompt_templates.title: Search a Topic
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner research on a broad question.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to search the web for recent sources about renewable energy storage. Return the main findings and source links.

prompt_templates.title: Extract One Article
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn a specific URL into clean text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Tavily extract on this article URL. Summarize the main points and list any images or source references found.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Search Providers
prompt_templates.scenario: Run the same topic through Tavily and Exa.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for enterprise vector database benchmarks with Tavily and Exa. Compare the strongest sources and note disagreements.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Research Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for synthesis with citations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent AI agent evaluation methods, extract the top three sources, then synthesize a cited executive brief.

output_examples.input: Search for best practices for building AI agents.
output_examples.output: A concise answer with relevant source links, short context for each source, and image references when available.

output_examples.input: Extract text from a long article URL.
output_examples.output: Clean article text, detected images, and a short summary ready for review or later model analysis.

output_examples.input: Ask Exa for a direct factual answer.
output_examples.output: A direct answer with supporting web context, suitable for follow-up verification.

best_practices: Use specific queries with dates, entities, and source quality requirements.
Review returned sources before using results in decisions or publications.
Avoid sending confidential URLs, credentials, or private documents to external providers.
anti_patterns: Do not treat provider answers as verified facts without checking sources.
Do not run extraction on private systems unless sharing is approved.
Do not use broad queries when a targeted research question is available.

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: It requires the inference.sh belt CLI and access to the configured Tavily, Exa, or model apps.

faq.question: Does this skill browse the web directly?
faq.answer: It uses belt to call remote inference.sh apps that perform search, answering, and extraction.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential research?
faq.answer: Use caution. Queries, URLs, and extracted content may be sent to external providers.

faq.question: Does it include automatic fact verification?
faq.answer: No. It returns provider results and sources, but users must verify important claims.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs an external CLI and sends data to network services, but no malicious behavior was found.
